audit_status.source_base: Greek Hebrews text tradition with Genesis 14 and Psalm 110 interpretation; draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for Melchizedek, Abraham tithe, Levi, change of priesthood, indestructible life, better hope, permanent priesthood.

verses.restored: For this Melchizedek, king of Salem, priest of Most High Divine, meeting Abraham returning from slaughter of kings and blessing him,
to whom also Abraham apportioned a tenth from all, first being interpreted king of righteousness/justice, and then also king of Salem, which is king of peace,
without father, without mother, without genealogy, having neither beginning of days nor end of life, but being made like the Son of the Divine, remains priest continually.
Now see how great this one is, to whom Abraham the patriarch gave a tenth from the best spoils.
And those from sons of Levi receiving priesthood have command according to Law to take tithes from the people, that is, from their siblings, though these have come out of Abraham's loins.
But the one not tracing genealogy from them received tithes from Abraham and blessed the one having the promises.
And without all dispute, the lesser is blessed by the greater.
And here dying humans receive tithes, but there one testified that he lives.
And so to speak, through Abraham even Levi, the one receiving tithes, has paid tithes,
for he was still in the loins of the father when Melchizedek met him.

Therefore if completion were through the Levitical priesthood, for upon it the people received Law, what further need was there for another priest to arise according to order of Melchizedek and not be named according to order of Aaron?
For priesthood being changed, of necessity there is also change of Law.
For the one of whom these things are said has shared in another tribe, from which no one has attended the altar.
For it is evident that our Lord has arisen from Judah, concerning which tribe Moses spoke nothing about priests.
And it is still more abundantly clear if another priest arises according to likeness of Melchizedek,
who has become so not according to Law of fleshly commandment, but according to power of indestructible life.
For it is testified: You are priest into the age according to order of Melchizedek.
For on one hand there is setting aside of preceding command because of its weakness and unprofitability,
for Law completed nothing, but there is bringing in of better hope through which we draw near to the Divine.
And inasmuch as not apart from oath—others indeed have become priests without oath,
but he with oath through the One saying to him: Lord swore and will not change mind: You are priest into the age—
by so much also Jesus has become guarantor of better covenant.
And the many have become priests because they are hindered by death from remaining,
but this one, because he remains into the age, holds priesthood untransferable/permanent.
Therefore he is able to save/heal completely those drawing near to the Divine through him, always living to intercede on their behalf.
For such high priest was fitting for us: holy, harmless, undefiled, separated from sinners/misaligned ones, and become higher than heavens,
who does not have daily necessity, like the high priests, first to offer sacrifices for own sins/misalignments and then for those of people; for this he did once for all, offering himself.
For Law appoints humans as high priests having weakness, but word of oath after the Law appoints Son, completed into the age.

translation_notes: Melchizedek is interpreted through name, genealogy absence, and Psalm 110; avoid speculative mythology beyond the argument.
Change of priesthood/law is a Hebrews-internal cultic argument, not a rejection of Jewish people or Scripture.
Jesus' priesthood is grounded in indestructible life and ongoing intercession.
safety_note: Do not weaponize 'weakness/unprofitability' language as contempt for Torah or Judaism; Hebrews argues about priestly completion and access to Divine, using Scripture itself.
